governing_requirement: Main must normalize only eligible, exact-scope-compatible Mode A and Mode B receipts, independently revalidate authority/QA/freshness, compare preservation risk and operational cost under a pinned policy, deterministically choose, branch, or abstain, and never compare incompatible latent representations or weaken the pass requirement.

ownership_boundary.MaskFactory: Define receipt authority semantics, publish exact producer evidence, and validate a Main arbitration decision against current producer certificates/revocations and frozen contracts.
ownership_boundary.Comfy_UI_Main: Own normalization into controller candidates, arbitration policy execution, DAG branching/abstention, and the signed consumer decision.
ownership_boundary.prohibited: MaskFactory cannot choose or sign Main's controller route, and Main cannot upgrade MaskFactory authority.

prerequisites: MF-P6-11.02 has no accepted immutable Mode A read decision.
MF-P6-11.03 has no accepted contract-native Mode B exchange.
No signed Main arbitration decision or accepted normalization policy is available for producer conformance validation.
verified_foundations: The frozen receipt schema provides exact access mode, media/source/subject/release/provider/artifact/transform/QA/authority/truth/lineage/use-eligibility identities and execution resource/timing observations.

Receipt validation enforces trusted producer signature, canonical hash, authentication, media identity, timing/lease coherence, QA/result honesty, artifact/source/transform binding, lineage, parent authority ceiling, exact-output certification, and use-eligibility authority floor.
The authoritative plan explicitly says Mode A access alone grants no rank, exact wrapper-certified Mode A outranks an uncertified Mode B draft for the same scope, and newer does not imply better.
The contracts exchange explicit masks and decoded artifact identities rather than shared incompatible latent state.
Focused positive and authority-ceiling receipt tests pass.

commands: in-memory validation and exact-scope comparison of positive Mode A and certified Mode B fixture receipts

same_scope_fixture_probe.interpretation: Individual validity does not normalize candidates or decide between them; no pair/set validator, policy hash, comparison trace, tie threshold, branch, or abstention record exists.

confirmed_gaps: No receipt normalizer or arbitration implementation exists for Mode A/Mode B results.
No closed comparable-scope identity proves equality of project/run/job/pass/intent/source/media/owner/instance/ontology/transform/target/protected/use requirements before ranking.

No current-state eligibility phase rechecks exact operational certificate, release/capability/provider/policy/ontology/revocation freshness and independently recomputes intended-use authority before comparison.

Receipt QA uncertainty and execution resource/timing fields exist, but no pinned policy converts QA, freshness, preservation risk, latency/resource cost, and pass constraints into deterministic normalized dimensions.
The receipt has no preservation-risk observation or arbitration policy/decision binding; a caller could invent weights or silently favor novelty/newness.
No dominance rule proves wrapper-certified exact Mode A wins over an uncertified Mode B draft for identical scope regardless of draft score/cost.
No deterministic epsilon/tie/ambiguity policy chooses one, branches bounded alternatives, or abstains, and no limit prevents incompatible latent/artifact types from being compared.
No signed decision trace records eligible/rejected candidates, normalized values, dominance eliminations, tie handling, selected/branched/abstained outcome, and exact producer/consumer heads.

No tests cover newer-but-stale, high-QA-but-weaker-authority, lower-cost-but-insufficient, preservation-risk dominance, close alternatives, incompatible scope/latent type, or producer/Main eligibility disagreement.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement an additive producer-side arbitration conformance oracle and evidence validator that verifies a signed Main normalization/arbitration decision against exact receipts and current producer authority without executing or signing Main routing.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. Candidate paths are read-only. The scope is disjoint from frozen v1 contracts, preserved MF-P6-08.05 dirty paths, and prepared MF-P6-11.01 through 11.03 paths.
acceptance_criteria: Accept only trusted valid receipts and a trusted signed Main arbitration decision pinned to exact receipt hashes, current producer release/capability/certificate/revocation heads, consumer requirements, intended-use policy, and normalization policy hash.

Partition by an exact comparable-scope identity covering project/run/job/pass/intent, source/media, owner/person/instance/revision, ontology, transform, target/protected regions, artifact kind/coordinate space, and required use/authority; incompatible scopes never rank together.

Independently recompute current eligibility and reject stale/revoked/out-of-scope/insufficient/malformed candidates before scoring; producer use_eligibility is an observation, not consumer decision authority.

Normalize QA, preservation risk, evidence freshness, latency/resource cost, and pass constraints under closed units, ranges, missing-value rules, directionality, weights, dominance, epsilon, and budget policy.
Enforce authority dominance: exact actively wrapper-certified Mode A outranks an uncertified Mode B draft for identical scope, while Mode A access/status/reference alone has no authority advantage.
Newness, lower cost, or a higher advisory score cannot overcome insufficient authority, failed QA, stale evidence, protected-region risk, or unmet pass requirements.
Close alternatives deterministically produce bounded branch or abstention according to policy; arbitrary input order, float representation, or provider identity cannot change the decision.
Reject incompatible latent comparisons; only explicit decoded artifacts/masks with exact coordinate/semantic bindings may enter cross-engine arbitration.

Emit a strict self-hashed conformance record with accepted/rejected candidates, normalized dimensions, dominance/tie trace, decision, producer/Main heads, and deterministic reasons without signing Main route state.

Tests cover all required dominance, ambiguity, order-invariance, freshness, authority disagreement, partial scope, cost, preservation, incompatible scope/latent, and no-silent-weakening cases while frozen contracts remain byte-identical.

claim_boundary: This audit credits individual receipt identity, authority, QA, lineage, timing, resource, and eligibility validation only. It does not normalize candidates, compare receipts, execute or validate a Main choice/branch/abstention, or complete MF-P6-11.04.
